channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Venezuela Residents Describe Terror as Deadly Earthquake Struck
item.description: Buildings collapsed, windows rattled and residents poured into the streets in Venezuela’s capital and nearby cities after two major earthquakes hit.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky, Tibisay Romero, Fabiola Ferrero, Julie Turkewitz, María Victoria Fermín and Yan Zhuang

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A couple cries in front of a building that collapsed during the earthquakes that struck Venezuela on Wednesday.

item.title: North Korea Deploys Its First Destroyer, Vowing to Project Nuclear Power by Sea
item.description: The 5,000-ton Choe Hyon is the largest warship North Korea has ever built and is said to be armed with nuclear-capable missiles.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:credit: Korean Central News Agency, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A photo released by North Korean state media is said to show its leader, Kim Jong-un, center, at the commissioning ceremony of a new destroyer, the Choe Hyon, at Nampo port on Tuesday.

item.title: Donde los multimillonarios veranean, trabajadores migrantes enfrentan fríos mortales
item.description: La vida dura de un paisajista guatemalteco y su solitaria muerte revelan el costo humano que entraña el cuidado paisaje de los Hamptons.
item.dc:creator: Rukmini Callimachi and Victor J. Blue

media:content.media:description: El paisajista Gilberto Camey sale del bosque de camino a su trabajo diario, recorriendo los pasos que siguió su hermano antes de que lo encontraran muerto tras una tormenta de invierno.

item.title: El México-Irlanda fue el partido más caluroso de la historia del Mundial: ‘Una locura’
item.description: Jugadores exhaustos, aficionados desmayados y bolsitas de agua lanzadas desde la banda. Así fue aquel encuentro de 1994 en Orlando, considerado uno de los más calurosos en la historia de los Mundiales.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Alain Gadoffre/Onze/Icon Sport, vía Getty Images
media:content.media:description: México venció a Irlanda por 2-1 después de que el calor y la humedad extremos afectaran especialmente a los irlandeses.

item.title: Jimmy Fallon Teases Trump’s Great American State Fair
item.description: “There’s even a dunk tank with JD Vance and a drunk tank with Kash Patel,” Fallon said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Mamdani’s Victories May Be a Headache for the Governor Who Has Backed Him
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul has worked with Mayor Zohran Mamdani on child care and second-home taxes. But the triumphs of democratic socialists on Primary Day could complicate her vision.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Shannon Stapleton/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul celebrated the victory of Micah Lasher, an establishment candidate.

item.title: After Banner Night, D.S.A. Sharpens Its Policy and Political Ambitions
item.description: After democratic socialists captured two House primaries and several state legislative contests in New York, the group’s leaders mulled how to use their expanding influence.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A rally to push the Tax the Rich movement was held in Albany earlier this year, but Mayor Zohran Mamdani did not attend.

item.title: Trump Administration and the M.T.A. Clash Over Penn Station Redesign
item.description: President Trump has made revitalizing the cramped train hall a priority, but he will need the cooperation of a transit agency his administration has long criticized.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Today’s Pennsylvania Station is often regarded with contempt by commuters.

item.title: Why These Affordable Homes Face a 31% Rent Increase
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has promised relief for rent-stabilized tenants. But thousands of apartments under a separate city program are subject to increases, creating an awkward contrast.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri and Dana Rubinstein
item.category: Department of Housing Preservation and Development (NYC)

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tracey Towers in the Bronx is part of the Mitchell-Lama program, which created a decades-old bastion of middle-income and working-class housing in New York City.

item.title: Mamdani’s Rent Freeze Could Become Reality Today. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: A freeze for rent-stabilized homes is expected when a city panel votes on Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s plan on Thursday, but the move is likely to face legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s pledge to “freeze the rent” for rent-stabilized apartments was a popular rallying cry during his mayoral campaign.

item.title: Mamdani Ends Day Care Contract With Eric Adams’s Former Romantic Partner
item.description: The city had accused Jasmine Ray of fiscal mismanagement. She said that she had been unfairly targeted because of her ties to the former mayor.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag
item.category: Mayor's Office of Sports, Wellness and Recreation (NYC)

media:content.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Cornerstone Day Care Center, which opened in 1966, is among the oldest day cares in Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 25, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Venezuela’s Neighbors Offer Aid in Earthquake Response
item.description: Officials from the United States, El Salvador and the Dominican Republic said they would send rescue teams and humanitarian assistance to Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emergency services searching the rubble of a collapsed building on Wednesday.

item.title: Nueva York en alerta por un misterioso evento en el Madison Square Garden
item.description: El Times ha confirmado que Taylor Swift ha alquilado la arena deportiva para el fin de semana del 4 de julio, lo que sugiere que tiene previsto organizar una celebración de varios días.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman, Jonah E. Bromwich, Emma G. Fitzsimmons, Dana Rubinstein and Maria Cramer

media:content.media:credit: Gregory Shamus/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Desde que anunciaron su compromiso en agosto de 2025, ha habido intensas especulaciones sobre los detalles de la boda de Taylor Swift y Travis Kelce.

item.title: Word of the Day: apotheosis
item.description: This word has appeared in 44 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Oil Prices Fall Toward Prewar Levels as Gulf Shipping Resumes
item.description: Oil prices fell, moving closer to levels not seen since the war in Iran began in February.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Elke Scholiers/Getty Images

item.title: Así se ve una Europa agobiada por el calor
item.description: El calor extremo está trastocando la vida cotidiana en todo el continente europeo.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia

media:content.media:credit: Oscar Del Pozo/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: There’s One Clear Reason Why Americans Are Gloomy About A.I.
item.description: The key is understanding how our labor market and our social net differs from the rest of the world.
item.dc:creator: Paul Kedrosky

item.title: Sismo en Venezuela: el terremoto de 7,5 es el más fuerte en un siglo
item.description: El Servicio Geológico de EE. UU. calcula que podría ser mucho más mortífero que el terremoto de magnitud 7,7 que sacudió el país en 1900.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Suspect in Kansas City Shootings Found Dead in Basement, Police Say
item.description: Officers found the body of a man believed to be Oscar Sanchez-Munoz, the suspect in a series of roadway shootings last week, inside a home they had already searched.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

item.title: For America’s 250th, 16 Opinion Writers on the Country at Its Best
item.description: Two hundred and fifty years in, Opinion writers offer 16 reasons why the national experiment endures.
item.category: President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief

media:content.media:credit: Thomas Prior for The New York Times

item.title: To Escape the Heat, Parisians Jump into an Old Industrial Canal
item.description: Crowds are filling the Canal Saint-Martin in Paris, as temperatures soar above 100 degrees Fahrenheit. Forty people drowned while swimming in other waterways.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler and Ségolène Le Stradic

media:content.media:credit: Samuel Boivin/NurPhoto, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Swimming in Canal St. Martin in Paris on Tuesday.

item.title: La derecha de Estados Unidos se replantea su postura sobre Irán
item.description: El presidente Trump ha intentado proyectar una nueva imagen del gobierno iraní mientras busca un acuerdo de paz. Pero hay indicios de que el cambio de actitud del Partido Republicano hacia Irán va mucho más allá.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una manifestación a favor del gobierno en Teherán el mes pasado.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Trump Describes Venezuela as ‘Happy’. Its People Disagree.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, June 25, 2026.

item.title: A Puppy-Abuse Case in China, Caught on Video, Prompts Rare Public Protest
item.description: After a man was filmed torturing dogs, hundreds gathered in a rare demonstration that the police tried to break up. For many, the sit-in was their first civic action.
item.dc:creator: Murphy Zhao and Lily Kuo

media:content.media:credit: Video obtained by The New York Times

item.title: 7.5-Magnitude Quake Is Largest to Hit Venezuela in More Than a Century
item.description: The U.S. Geological Survey estimated that it could be far deadlier than a 7.7-magnitude earthquake that struck the country in 1900.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s D.C. Construction Spree Could Cost More Than $1 Billion
item.description: There has been no complete public accounting of the president’s construction spree, so The Times pieced one together for projects with known estimates.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Marco Hernandez, Junho Lee and Elena Shao
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Marco Hernandez / The New York Times

item.title: After Trump’s Outburst, Senate G.O.P. Reverses Course on Iran
item.description: In a late-night vote aimed at mollifying the president, Senate Republicans rejected a resolution directing him to end the war against Iran, a day after a bipartisan rebuke.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Michael Gold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump traveled to the Capitol for a tense closed-door lunch with Senate Republicans on Wednesday.

item.title: Representative Tom Kean, Missing for Months, Is Back Home in New Jersey
item.description: The congressman, who has been absent from Washington since March, answered the door of his home on Wednesday evening. He was wearing a suit and tie.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr., absent from Congress for nearly four months, was back at his New Jersey home on Wednesday evening.

item.title: What Does a Knicks Trash Can Cost? $168, or for One Woman, Her Job.
item.description: After a video of Angie Baez taking a Knicks-themed trash can went viral, a JPMorgan Chase spokeswoman said she was no longer with the company.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman and Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The New York Knicks’ championship parade last week in Manhattan.

item.title: Los venezolanos del sur de Florida llaman a sus familiares y organizan la ayuda
item.description: La numerosa comunidad venezolana de la zona de Miami está “desesperada” por ponerse en contacto con sus familiares.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

media:content.media:credit: Manaure Quintero/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Escombros y un reencuentro en una calle de Caracas el miércoles.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 25, 2026
item.description: There’s some shady business happening in Tarun Krishnamurthy’s puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: I scream, you scream, we all scream for puzzle-based intellectual stimulation.

item.title: The World Cup of Jerseys
item.description: 48 countries competing. 96 jerseys on display. Vote for your favorite designs and see how they rank with Times readers.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Morris and Nico Chilla

media:content.media:credit: Clockwise from top left: Dale Zanine/Imagn Images, Carl De Souza/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images, Matt Slocum/Associated Press, via Ap Photo/Matt Slocum, Travis Register/Imagn Images, via Imagn Images Via Reuters Connect, Alexander Hassenstein/Getty Images, Nick Wass/Associated Press, Eloisa Sanchez/Reuters, Mike Stewart/Associated Press

item.title: Venezuela Rocked by Two Strong Earthquakes
item.description: A 7.2-magnitude earthquake that was followed by a 7.5-magnitude earthquake hit central Venezuela on Wednesday, damaging buildings and knocking out electricity.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: Associated Press

item.title: Venezuelans in South Florida Call Relatives and Organize Aid
item.description: The large Venezuelan community in the Miami area is “desperate” to reach family members.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

media:content.media:credit: Manaure Quintero/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Wreckage and a reunion on a street in Caracas on Wednesday.

item.title: Dos terremotos sacuden a Venezuela
item.description: Después de que dos fuertes sismos se registraran en el país sudamericano, crece la preocupación de que los daños puedan ser devastadores.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Juan Barreto/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Equipos de rescate sacan a una persona en camilla de un edificio colapsado tras el terremoto del miércoles en Caracas.

item.title: Ex-Youth Pastor Charged With Wife’s Murder, 20 Years After Her Fatal Fall
item.description: Bernadette Vander Meer’s death in Zion National Park in Utah had been considered an accident. Prosecutors reopened the case in 2022 and now say her husband was having an affair with an underage girl.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:credit: TBA for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The view from the summit of the Angels Landing Trail in Zion National Park, where Bernadette Vander Meer died in 2006.

item.title: Queens State Assembly Candidate Charged With Forgery for A.I. Use
item.description: The arraignment on Wednesday is said to be the first time a politician in New York is facing criminal charges relating to A.I. interference in an election.
item.dc:creator: Miles G. Cohen

media:content.media:credit: Jeenah Moon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A prosecutor accused Jonathan Rinaldi of using artificial intelligence to “advance the selfish goal of winning a City Council seat by any means.”

item.title: Judge Throws Out Federal Suit Against 4 N.J. ‘Sanctuary’ Cities
item.description: Judge Evelyn Padin ruled the Justice Department’s lawsuit ​was flawed because it did not also challenge a state directive limiting local cooperation with federal immigration agents.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Newark police officers at a protest outside Delaney Hall this month.

item.title: Avila Chevalier Won Despite Far-Left Views. Will They Hurt Democrats?
item.description: Darializa Avila Chevalier’s questionable social media history and outlier politics are already being highlighted by Republican leaders as they look to November.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Darializa Avila Chevalier was one of three House candidates, all backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who won primary battles on Tuesday.

item.title: Photos: Earthquakes Strike Venezuela
item.description: After two powerful quakes shook the South American country, concern is growing that the damage could be devastating.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emergency workers attempting to rescue people trapped in the rubble of a residential building that collapsed during an earthquake in Caracas, Venezuela, on Wednesday.

item.title: NATO’s Leader Makes His Case to Trump for Preserving the Alliance
item.description: Using charts, Secretary General Mark Rutte appeared to defuse the president’s anger by showing that European countries were “equalizing” defense spending with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mark Rutte, the secretary general of NATO, with President Trump and other officials in the Oval Office on Wednesday. The organization’s summit starts on July 7 in Turkey.

item.title: Incertidumbre tras dos fuertes sismos
item.description: Los temblores de magnitud 7,2 y 7,5 derrumbaron edificios y desencadenaron operaciones de rescate en Caracas y otras localidades. EE. UU. y varios países latinoamericanos anunciaron que estaban movilizando ayuda.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times

item.title: Dolly Parton’s Fans Were Worried. She Came to a Truck Stop to See Them.
item.description: Fans have been concerned about the singer’s health since she postponed shows last year. So some were thrilled to catch a glimpse of her on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:credit: Landon Edwards for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fans wait in line to place an order at Dolly’s Tennessean Travel Stop’s coffee bar on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Claims He Asked Prosecutor to ‘Do Me a Favor’ and Look Into the California Election
item.description: The president took credit for the advance of the former Fox News host, Steve Hilton, to the runoff for governor.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump claimed that a call he made to a U.S. attorney in California helped Steve Hilton advance to the runoff for governor. Mr. Hilton said he had no knowledge of any such call.

item.title: Meta pone la mira en los mercados de predicción
item.description: Una aplicación experimental, llamada internamente Arena, iría tras los clientes de Polymarket y Kalshi.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac and David Yaffe-Bellany

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mark Zuckerberg, director ejecutivo de Meta, está impulsando de forma más amplia la creación de nuevos tipos de aplicaciones basadas en comportamientos sociales emergentes en internet.

item.title: In New York Election Results, More Evidence of Eroding Support for Israel
item.description: If the shift in public opinion continues, it could reshape one of the United States’ closest alliances.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Jennifer Medina
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York campaigning with Claire Valdez. “We will stand up to the genocide,” Ms. Valdez told supporters after winning her Democratic House primary on Tuesday.

item.title: California’s Plastic Law Goes to Court: Is It Too Strict or Too Lax?
item.description: The sweeping legislation, fully enacted last month, has been challenged by Republican-led states. Environmental groups have their own complaints.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:credit: Rich Pedroncelli/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: California’s law requires that manufacturers reduce the amount of plastic in single-use items such as shampoo bottles by reducing package sizing, switching to a different material or making the products reusable.
